title: [Docs] Missing REST API reference documentation for HTTP endpoints
Redact sensitive headers before sending request context to Sentry
description:
Global exception handler sends full request headers to Sentry context. This may include Authorization tokens and cookies, creating secret leakage risk in observability tooling.

Evidence:
- ace_platform/api/main.py generic exception handler sets context with dict(request.headers)

Acceptance:
- Add header allowlist or explicit redaction for Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie, API keys, and similar secrets
- Add test verifying redaction behavior in captured Sentry context
- Document telemetry redaction policy

title: [Docs] No search functionality configured for docs site
Add IP allowlist support for API keys
description:
## Problem
API keys can be used from any IP address. If a key is compromised, attacker has full access.

## Solution
Allow users to restrict API keys to specific IP addresses or ranges.

## Implementation
1. Add `allowed_ips` field to APIKey model (JSON array)
2. On API key creation, optionally specify allowed IPs/CIDRs
3. On each request, validate source IP against allowlist
4. If allowlist is empty, allow any IP (current behavior)

## UI/UX
- Show current IP to user when creating key
- Allow CIDR notation (e.g., 10.0.0.0/8)
- Warn if adding very broad ranges

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/db/models.py` - add field to APIKey
- `ace_platform/api/auth.py` - validate IP on key auth
- `ace_platform/api/routes/auth.py` - accept IPs on creation

title: [UX] External Google Fonts dependency - no fallback font-display strategy
description: globals.css uses @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?...') for Playfair Display, Cormorant Garamond, and Fira Code. This is a render-blocking external dependency. If Google Fonts is slow or unavailable (corporate firewalls, certain regions), the UI will use system fallbacks but the font-display strategy in the URL only uses 'display=swap' which can cause FOIT/FOUT. Consider self-hosting the fonts or using font-display:optional to prevent layout shift.

title: Align UAT/API docs with implemented endpoints and payloads
description:
Launch docs currently contain several contract mismatches that will cause failed QA and customer confusion.

Examples:
- docs/UAT_CHECKLIST.md register payload includes username, but API register schema is email+password
- docs/UAT_CHECKLIST.md uses PATCH /playbooks/{id}, API implements PUT
- docs/UAT_CHECKLIST.md uses outcome_status field, API expects outcome
- docs/UAT_CHECKLIST.md + docs/QUICKSTART.md reference /playbooks/{id}/evolve and /evolution-status endpoints not present in REST routes
- docs/API_REFERENCE.md lists playbook status filter values including draft, while model uses paused

Acceptance:
- Update docs to match current API behavior
- Add docs validation checklist to release process
- Verify examples against automated API smoke tests

title: Add Stripe webhook idempotency tracking
description:
Webhook handler processes events directly without persisting processed event IDs. Stripe retries can deliver duplicate events, causing duplicate side effects risk (repeated updates/audit noise).

Evidence:
- ace_platform/core/webhooks.py dispatches by event.type with no event.id dedupe store

Acceptance:
- Persist processed Stripe event IDs with unique constraint
- Skip already-processed events safely
- Add tests for duplicate webhook delivery

title: Add anomaly detection for suspicious access patterns
description:
## Problem
No behavioral analysis to detect unusual access patterns that might indicate compromise or abuse.

## Solution
Add basic anomaly detection for suspicious activity.

## Patterns to detect
- Login from new geographic location
- Unusual time of access
- Rapid API key creation
- High volume of failed operations
- Accessing many different playbooks rapidly
- Sudden spike in usage

## Implementation
1. Track user behavior baselines (location, time, usage patterns)
2. Flag anomalies for review or automatic action
3. Send notification emails for suspicious activity
4. Consider temporary account lock pending verification

## Simpler alternative for MVP
- Just send email on login from new IP/location
- Alert yourself when any user's spend exceeds threshold

## Notes
This is nice-to-have. Focus on preventive measures first (email verification, spending caps).

title: [Backend] JWT default secret key is insecure placeholder

Many interactive buttons lack aria-labels: sidebar nav toggle, logout button, delete buttons on API key cards, setup docs button, OAuth buttons, modal close buttons, tab buttons in PlaybookDetail, filter select. Important for screen reader users.

title: Add device/session management
description:
## Problem
- No tracking of active sessions or devices
- No way to see where you're logged in
- Can't revoke specific sessions
- JWT tokens can't be invalidated before expiry

## Solution
Add session tracking and management.

## Implementation
1. Create Session model:
   - id, user_id, token_hash, device_info
   - ip_address, user_agent, created_at, last_used_at
   - is_revoked
2. Store session on login, update last_used on each request
3. Add endpoints:
   - `GET /auth/sessions` - list active sessions
   - `DELETE /auth/sessions/{id}` - revoke specific session
   - `DELETE /auth/sessions` - revoke all except current
4. Check session validity on each authenticated request
5. Add Redis-based token blocklist for immediate revocation

## Tradeoffs
- Adds database lookup on every request (can cache in Redis)
- More complex auth flow
- Consider if really needed for your user base

title: Add partial unique index for evolution concurrency
description:
Add partial unique index on EvolutionJob table to prevent concurrent evolutions:

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_evolution_job_active_per_playbook 
ON evolution_jobs (playbook_id) 
WHERE status IN ('queued', 'running');

This ensures only one active evolution job per playbook at a time.

title: Implement automatic evolution triggering
description:
Implement automatic evolution triggering based on thresholds:

Rules:
- Trigger after N unprocessed outcomes (default: 5)
- OR after T hours since last evolution with at least 1 outcome (default: 24h)
- Whichever condition is met first
- Outcomes arriving during a running job wait for next run

Implement as periodic Celery beat task.

title: Implement password reset flow
description:
## Problem
No password reset mechanism exists. Users who forget their password have no recovery path.

## Solution
Implement secure password reset with email verification.

## Implementation
1. Add `/auth/forgot-password` endpoint - accepts email, sends reset link
2. Add `/auth/reset-password` endpoint - accepts token + new password
3. Generate secure reset tokens (cryptographic random, short expiry)
4. Store token hash in database (not plaintext)
5. Token expiry: 1 hour
6. Invalidate token after use
7. Rate limit reset requests (3 per hour per email)

## Security considerations
- Don't reveal whether email exists (same response either way)
- Use timing-safe comparison for tokens
- Log all reset attempts for audit

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/api/routes/auth.py`
- `ace_platform/db/models.py` - add PasswordResetToken model
- Add email sending utility
close_reason: Already implemented - forgot-password and reset-password endpoints exist

title: Add request correlation ID middleware
description:
Add middleware to generate and propagate correlation IDs:

1. Generate UUID for each request if not present in headers
2. Add to response headers
3. Include in all log messages
4. Pass through to background tasks

Essential for debugging and tracing requests across services.

title: Deploy MCP server

Deploy MCP server.

Architecture decision: Consider hosting MCP as part of FastAPI app to reduce operational complexity. If combined: single deployment with MCP endpoints on separate router. If separate: deploy as additional process.

description: Deploy Redis using Upstash or Fly

title: Add two-factor authentication (2FA) support
description:
## Problem
High-value accounts are vulnerable to credential compromise. No second factor of authentication.

## Solution
Add optional 2FA using TOTP (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.)

## Implementation
1. Add 2FA fields to User model:
   - `totp_secret` (encrypted)
   - `totp_enabled`
   - `backup_codes` (hashed)
2. Add endpoints:
   - `POST /auth/2fa/setup` - generate secret, return QR code
   - `POST /auth/2fa/verify` - verify code, enable 2FA
   - `POST /auth/2fa/disable` - disable with password confirmation
   - `GET /auth/2fa/backup-codes` - generate backup codes
3. Modify login flow to require 2FA code when enabled
4. Add backup codes for recovery

## Libraries
- `pyotp` for TOTP generation/verification
- `qrcode` for QR code generation

## Alternative
Consider just pushing users toward Google/GitHub OAuth which provides 2FA at the provider level - simpler and shifts security responsibility.
close_reason: Skipped - OAuth providers (Google/GitHub) already provide 2FA at their level, making native TOTP implementation lower priority

title: Fix evolution status enum mismatch between API and frontend
description:
Backend evolution jobs use status=queued|running|completed|failed, while frontend EvolutionJob type and Playbook detail status styles expect pending|running|completed|failed. This causes status rendering mismatch for queued jobs and can break styling logic.

Evidence:
- ace_platform/db/models.py EvolutionJobStatus uses QUEUED
- web/src/types/index.ts EvolutionJob uses pending
- web/src/pages/PlaybookDetail/PlaybookDetail.tsx statusStyles uses pending

Acceptance:
- Unified enum values across backend responses and frontend types/components
- Add FE test for queued/active job status rendering
- Update docs/examples to match canonical values

title: Add CSRF protection for state-changing operations
description:
## Problem
No CSRF tokens for state-changing operations. Session-based endpoints (especially OAuth) may be vulnerable.

## Current mitigating factors
- API uses token-based auth primarily (not cookies)
- CORS configured to limit origins

## Solution
Add CSRF protection for any cookie/session-based flows.

## Implementation
1. Generate CSRF token on session creation
2. Require token in header or body for state-changing requests
3. Validate token matches session
4. Focus on OAuth flow which uses sessions

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/api/routes/oauth.py`
- `ace_platform/api/middleware.py`

## Notes
Lower priority since most auth is token-based, but OAuth flow should be protected.

title: Add ApiKey model for MCP authentication
description:
Add ApiKey table for per-user MCP API keys:
- id: UUID (primary key)
- user_id: UUID (foreign key, indexed)
- name: str
- key_prefix: str (first 8 chars for identification)
- hashed_key: str (bcrypt hashed, never store plaintext)
- scopes: JSONB
- created_at: datetime
- last_used_at: datetime (nullable)
- revoked_at: datetime (nullable)

Keys must be hashed like passwords. Only show full key once at creation time.

Acceptance:

title: Test evolution idempotency
description:
Add E2E test for evolution idempotency:

1. Trigger evolution for a playbook
2. While job is queued/running, trigger again
3. Verify same job_id returned (is_new=false)
4. Verify no duplicate jobs created

Critical for preventing race conditions.

title: Create evolution worker task
description:
Create Celery evolution worker task with:

1. Sync database session (not async)
2. Row locking via SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on playbook
3. Atomic transaction for:
   - Create new PlaybookVersion
   - Update Playbook.current_version_id
   - Mark outcomes as processed with evolution_job_id
   - Update EvolutionJob status
4. Token usage logging to UsageRecord
5. Retry with exponential backoff
6. Idempotent behavior

title: Add admin alerting for high-spend users
description:
## Problem
As an indie developer, you need to know immediately when something looks wrong - before costs spiral.

## Solution
Add simple alerting when users hit spend thresholds.

## Implementation
1. Add threshold checks in metering code
2. Alert when any user:
   - Hits 50% of their tier's cost limit
   - Hits 80% of their tier's cost limit
   - Has unusual spike vs their baseline
3. Send alert via:
   - Email to admin
   - Slack webhook (optional)
   - Log with high severity for monitoring

## Simple MVP
Just add a daily summary email:
- Total platform spend today
- Top 5 users by spend
- Any users who exceeded 50% of limit

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/core/metering.py` - add threshold checks
- `ace_platform/workers/tasks.py` - add daily summary task
- Add email/notification utility

title: Fix current frontend ESLint errors blocking release quality gate
description:
Current web lint fails with 6 errors, including unused vars and setState-in-effect violations. This should be resolved before launch and before enabling strict frontend CI gates.

Evidence:

- web/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx:84 react-refresh export issue

The fly.toml release_command 'alembic upgrade head' will fail if migrations aren't bundled. Verify the pip install -e . includes them or add an explicit COPY for the migrations directory.

title: [Docs] Pricing/plan tables inconsistent between core-concepts, understanding-evolution, and authentication pages
Investigate MCP session disconnection causing -32602 errors
description:
## Problem

Users experience intermittent MCP session disconnections that result in `-32602: Invalid request parameters` JSON-RPC errors. The only workaround is manually running `/mcp` to reconnect.

## Symptoms

- MCP tools stop working mid-session
- Error: `MCP error -32602: Invalid request parameters`
- Reconnecting via `/mcp` fixes the issue temporarily
- Happens after some period of use/idle time

## Potential Causes

1. **Fly.io load balancer timeouts** - SSE connections may be dropped after idle period
2. **Machine replacements during deploys** - Rolling deploys drop active connections
3. **No SSE keepalive/ping** - Connections timeout without client awareness
4. **Client-side session caching** - Claude Code may cache stale session state
5. **Database session issues** - Single AsyncSession in mcp_lifespan could become stale

## Current Config

- `min_machines_running = 1` (always 1 API machine)
- `auto_stop_machines = "stop"` (affects additional machines)
- No explicit SSE keepalive configuration
- FastMCP 1.26.0

## Investigation Steps

1. [ ] Check Fly.io load balancer timeout settings
2. [ ] Add SSE keepalive pings if FastMCP supports it
3. [ ] Test if issue occurs with local MCP server (isolate server vs client)
4. [ ] Review Claude Code MCP client reconnection behavior
5. [ ] Consider adding connection health checks

## Impact

Poor user experience - users must manually reconnect during workflow.

title: [Docs] docs.aceagent.io custom domain may not be configured on Fly.io
close_reason: Infrastructure-only issue (Fly.io DNS), not a docs fix

6. Verify auto-evolution triggers at threshold
7. Verify version history created
8. View playbook versions in dashboard

Test with both REST API and MCP tools.

title: Add semantic playbook matching for automatic selection
description:
## Problem

When a user has multiple playbooks, agents must manually decide which playbook to use based on names and descriptions. This requires good naming conventions and relies on agent judgment.

## Proposed Solution

Add a `find_playbook` MCP tool that uses semantic matching to automatically select the best playbook for a given task:

```
find_playbook(task_description: string) -> playbook_id
```

### Implementation approach:

1. Generate embeddings for playbook content (on create/update)
2. Store embeddings in the database (pgvector or separate vector store)
3. When `find_playbook` is called:
   - Generate embedding for task description
   - Find nearest playbook by cosine similarity
   - Return best match (with confidence score)

### API additions:

- New MCP tool: `find_playbook`
- Optional: `list_playbooks` could accept a `task` param to return sorted by relevance

## Alternatives considered

- Tags/categories (requires manual tagging)
- Keyword matching (less accurate than semantic)
- Agent-side configuration (more setup burden on users)

title: Set up production environment variables
description: Configure all environment variables in production environment

title: Add security headers middleware
description:
## Problem
Missing standard security headers that browsers use for protection.

## Solution
Add middleware to set security headers on all responses.

## Headers to add
- `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains` (HSTS)
- `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`
- `X-Frame-Options: DENY`
- `X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block`
- `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`
- `Content-Security-Policy: default-src 'self'` (adjust as needed for API)

## Implementation
1. Create middleware in `ace_platform/api/middleware.py`
2. Add to middleware stack in `ace_platform/api/main.py`
3. Make headers configurable (some may need adjustment for frontend)

## Notes
- Less critical for API-only service but still good practice
- HSTS especially important for production

title: [UX] Unused dependencies: date-fns and react-markdown in package.json
description: date-fns and react-markdown are listed in package.json dependencies but are never imported anywhere in web/src/. Custom formatRelativeTime/formatTimeAgo helpers are used instead of date-fns, and PlaybookRenderer uses custom parsing instead of react-markdown. These add ~100KB+ to node_modules. Remove to reduce install time and bundle scanning.

title: Rate limit account creation per IP
description:
## Problem
No rate limit on account creation allows rapid creation of throwaway accounts from a single IP.

## Solution
Add rate limiting to the registration endpoint.

## Implementation
1. Add rate limit: 3 accounts per hour per IP
2. Use existing Redis-backed rate limiting infrastructure in `ace_platform/core/rate_limit.py`
3. Return 429 with clear message about limit

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/api/routes/auth.py` - add rate limit decorator to register endpoint
- `ace_platform/core/rate_limit.py` - add REGISTER limit config if needed

## Impact
Prevents rapid-fire account creation for abuse.
close_reason: Already implemented - RateLimitRegister applied to register endpoint

Create comprehensive documentation of all environment variables:

- Required vs optional
- Default values

Add to docs/ directory.

title: [UX] No meta description or OG tags for SEO/social sharing
description: index.html has only a title tag. Missing meta description, Open Graph tags (og:title, og:description, og:image), and Twitter card meta tags. This affects SEO and how the site appears when shared on social media.

title: [Backend] Deprecated sentry_sdk.push_scope used in exception handler
description: In ace_platform/api/main.py:327, sentry_sdk.push_scope() is deprecated and will be removed in the next major Sentry SDK version. Replace with the new Sentry scope API per migration guide.

title: [UX] No React Error Boundary - unhandled errors show white screen
description: The app has no React Error Boundary. If any component throws an unhandled error during rendering, the entire app will crash to a white screen with no recovery path. A top-level ErrorBoundary should catch render errors and show a friendly fallback UI with a 'reload' button. This is especially important for production where network errors, unexpected API responses, or edge cases can cause render failures.

title: Enforce email verification for protected routes
description:
## Implementation Complete

### Phase 1: Enforcement (Done)
- Updated `create_api_key` endpoint to require `VerifiedUser` instead of `RequiredUser`
- Added email verification check to MCP `trigger_evolution` tool
- Added email verification check to MCP `record_outcome` tool  
- Added email verification check to REST API `create_outcome` endpoint

### Phase 2: Email Flow (Done)
- Added Resend email service integration
- Created `ace_platform/core/email.py` with:
  - JWT-based verification tokens
  - `send_verification_email()` function
  - `send_welcome_email()` function
- Added new endpoints:
  - `POST /auth/send-verification-email` - resend verification email
  - `POST /auth/verify-email` - verify email with token
- Updated registration to send verification email on signup
- Added config settings for Resend API key and email settings
- Updated `.env.example` with email configuration
- Added `resend` to project dependencies

### OAuth Users
OAuth users (Google/GitHub) are automatically verified since their emails are verified by the provider.

### Files Modified
- `ace_platform/api/auth.py` - Added VerifiedUser import
- `ace_platform/api/routes/auth.py` - Email verification endpoints
- `ace_platform/api/routes/playbooks.py` - Outcome verification check
- `ace_platform/mcp/server.py` - MCP tool verification checks
- `ace_platform/core/email.py` - New email service module
- `ace_platform/config.py` - Email settings
- `.env.example` - Email configuration docs
- `pyproject.toml` - Added resend dependency

title: Implement API key management endpoints
description:
Add CRUD endpoints for API key management:

POST   /auth/api-keys          - Create new API key (returns full key once)
GET    /auth/api-keys          - List user's API keys (prefix only)
DELETE /auth/api-keys/{id}     - Revoke API key

Keys are hashed with bcrypt. Only the key_prefix is shown after creation.
Support scopes: read, write, evolve.

title: Add legal pages and links required for SaaS launch (Terms, Privacy)
description:
App routing/navigation currently has no customer-facing Terms of Service or Privacy Policy pages/links. This is generally required before paid launch and account creation in most jurisdictions and payment processors.

Evidence:
- web/src/App.tsx routes include auth/dashboard/pricing/settings but no legal routes
- web/src/components/Layout/Layout.tsx nav has no legal links
- API landing page in ace_platform/api/main.py footer has no legal links

Acceptance:
- Add Terms and Privacy pages (and Cookie policy if applicable)
- Link legal pages from auth/pricing/footer/nav
- Version and publish effective dates

title: Document token economics analysis
description: Run sample ACE evolution loop with token counting, document tokens per agent call, calculate cost per evolution, create pricing recommendations

title: Fix frontend/backend API key schema mismatch
description:
Frontend expects ApiKey fields key_preview and expires_at, but backend returns key_prefix and no expiration fields. This can produce blank key identifiers and inconsistent UI behavior in API key management.

Evidence:
- web/src/types/index.ts defines key_preview/expires_at
- web/src/pages/ApiKeys/ApiKeys.tsx renders apiKey.key_preview and apiKey.expires_at
- ace_platform/api/routes/auth.py ApiKeyInfo returns key_prefix, created_at, last_used_at, is_active

Acceptance:
- FE and BE contracts are aligned (either adapt FE mapping layer or update API schema)
- Add integration test asserting API key list payload shape consumed by UI
- Update docs/API examples accordingly

title: Implement get_playbook MCP tool
description:
Implement get_playbook MCP tool:

Parameters:
- playbook_id: str (required)
- section: str (optional) - filter to specific section
- version: int (optional) - get specific version (default: current)

Returns: Playbook content as structured text

Must enforce playbook ownership - only return playbooks owned by authenticated user or public starters.

title: Enforce production config validation for secrets
description:
Application has insecure defaults for JWT/session secrets and does not fail fast in production when defaults are used. A misconfigured deploy could ship with weak auth secrets.

Evidence:
- ace_platform/config.py default jwt_secret_key is 'change-me-in-production'
- session_secret_key falls back to jwt_secret_key in ace_platform/api/main.py
- no production-only validator that rejects default/weak values

Acceptance:
- Add startup/config validation that rejects insecure defaults when ENVIRONMENT=production
- Require explicit SESSION_SECRET_KEY in production
- Add tests for validation behavior

title: Implement usage-based billing
description:
DEFERRED TO POST-MVP: Usage-based billing adds complexity:
- Reconcile model pricing changes
- Handle retries and failed calls
- Decide prompt+completion vs total billing
- When to send usage to Stripe

For MVP: Use flat subscription + usage cap instead. Track usage for visibility but defer Stripe metered billing.

title: Implement MCP authentication middleware
description:
Implement MCP authentication middleware for API keys:

1. Look up hashed key in database (bcrypt comparison)
2. Validate key is not revoked (revoked_at is null)
3. Check scopes for requested operation (read, write, evolve)
4. Update last_used_at timestamp on successful auth
5. Return 401/403 with clear error messages

API keys are generated via /auth/api-keys endpoints.

title: [Backend] MCP transport_security allows all hosts and origins

- Ruff linting
- Ruff formatting check
- pytest with coverage
- Type checking (optional: mypy)
- Test database setup for CI

Also add pre-commit hooks configuration.

title: Implement trigger_evolution MCP tool
description:
Implement trigger_evolution MCP tool:

Parameters: playbook_id (required)
Returns: job_id, status, is_new

IMPORTANT: Must be idempotent - if a queued/running job exists, return that job instead of creating a new one. This prevents race conditions and duplicate evolution jobs.

title: Implement audit logging for security events
description:
## Problem
Security events are not formally audited. No way to investigate incidents or meet compliance requirements.

## Solution
Add structured audit logging for security-relevant events.

## Events to log
- Login success/failure (with IP, user agent)
- Password changes
- API key creation/revocation
- Email verification
- Password reset requests
- Account lockouts
- Permission denied events
- Subscription changes
- Admin actions

## Implementation
1. Create `ace_platform/core/audit.py` with audit log functions
2. Create AuditLog database model with:
   - timestamp, user_id, event_type, ip_address
   - user_agent, details (JSON), severity
3. Add audit calls to relevant endpoints
4. Add admin endpoint to query audit logs
5. Consider shipping to external logging service (optional)

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/db/models.py` - add AuditLog model
- `ace_platform/core/audit.py` - new file
- Various route files to add audit calls

title: Document MCP integration steps
description: Document MCP integration steps and create example agent configuration

title: Require card on file for free trial (Stripe setup intent)
description:
## Problem
Free tier has no friction - anyone can create unlimited accounts and abuse the platform.

## Solution
Require a valid payment method (credit card) even for free trials using Stripe Setup Intents ($0 authorization).

## Implementation
1. Create Stripe Setup Intent on signup
2. Require successful card validation before enabling evolutions
3. Store payment method for future conversion to paid
4. Consider allowing limited functionality (view-only) without card

## Benefits
- Biggest single deterrent to abuse
- Higher conversion rates (card already on file)
- Can charge for overages if needed

## Alternative
If card requirement is too aggressive, consider:
- Phone verification instead
- Hard limit of 3 total evolutions (not per month) for unverified accounts

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/api/routes/auth.py`
- `ace_platform/core/stripe_integration.py` (or create new)
- Frontend signup flow
close_reason: Already implemented - has_payment_method, setup-card endpoint, webhook handling

title: Set up docker-compose for local development
description: Create docker-compose.yml for complete local development environment

title: Add spending cap per subscription tier
description:
## Problem
Tier limits cap evolution runs but not actual LLM spend. A malicious user could craft expensive prompts and blow through the LLM budget.

## Solution
Add a hard spending limit per tier that blocks further LLM calls when exceeded.

## Implementation
1. Add `monthly_cost_limit_usd` to `TierLimits` dataclass in `ace_platform/core/limits.py`
2. Define limits per tier:
   - FREE: $1.00/month
   - STARTER: $20.00/month (2x their payment)
   - PRO: $60.00/month
   - ULTRA: $160.00/month
   - ENTERPRISE: configurable
3. Add `check_spending_limit()` function similar to `check_can_evolve()`
4. Call this check before any LLM operation

## Files to modify
- `ace_platform/core/limits.py`
- `ace_platform/core/llm_proxy.py` (or wherever LLM calls are made)

## Impact
Protects against runaway LLM costs from malicious or buggy usage.
